jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
jobs.fitExplanation: This role is a strong match for Michael's expertise in complex B2B SaaS products, specifically in finance-adjacent areas like analytics dashboards, financial modeling workflows, and data platforms. His experience in making complex systems delightful and tactile is highly relevant to transforming complex financial workflows into simple, powerful experiences. The role's emphasis on driving design direction and influencing stakeholders aligns with his leadership experience. As a hybrid role in NYC, it fits the location criteria, though a slight deduction is applied as remote is preferred. HiBob is a Series D company, meeting the company stage requirement.
jobs.jobDescription: HiBob is seeking a Senior Product Designer for their Bob Finance module in the NYC Metro area. This position involves leading design for analytics dashboards, financial modeling workflows, and data platform tools, aiming to transform complex financial concepts into intuitive B2B experiences. The designer will be responsible for driving design direction, influencing stakeholders, and applying strong user research skills across multiple domains. Key requirements include 5+ years of experience in product design/UX, with a portfolio demonstrating design leadership and end-to-end ownership of complex product areas, particularly in analytics, data-heavy interfaces, or financial/enterprise software. The role requires working in a hybrid environment (2 days a week in the office) and strong skills in interaction design, visual design, information architecture, and usability testing. There is also an opportunity for growth into a leadership role as the team scales.

jobs.location: Remote or Hybrid in New York, NY
jobs.fitExplanation: This role is an exceptional fit for Michael Crane. His deep experience in FinTech, payments, transactional flows, and marketplace infrastructure directly aligns with leading design for Gusto's mission-critical Payments & Risk Platform. The focus on complex financial workflows, systems thinking, and elevating visual/interaction design quality are core strengths Michael possesses. His background in visual systems and commitment to 'visual craft' bringing trust to complex systems is highly relevant. The role also requires cross-functional leadership and a long-term UX vision, matching Michael's strategic capabilities. Gusto is a Series E company, fulfilling the company stage requirement. jobs.jobDescription: Gusto is actively seeking a Senior Product Design Lead for their Payments & Risk Platform. This individual contributor role entails owning end-to-end product design for complex financial workflows, with the goal of ensuring seamless, secure, and reliable money movement while delivering exceptional user experiences at scale. Responsibilities include defining and driving the long-term UX vision for the Payments & Risk platform, leading design strategy, and enhancing the overall visual and interaction design quality. Candidates should possess 8-10+ years of product design experience in complex, technical, or large-scale product domains, with a portfolio that showcases strong UX and visual design craft. Expertise in systems thinking, financial workflows, and the ability to lead design direction and influence cross-functional partners are crucial. This position offers flexibility for either remote work or a hybrid arrangement in New York City.

jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
jobs.fitExplanation: This remote role is an excellent fit for Michael, emphasizing his strengths in B2B SaaS and Fintech, particularly in simplifying complex, data-heavy applications. The position requires a strategic leader who can advocate for users and drive design excellence at scale, aligning perfectly with Michael's ability to shape design strategy and operate across the full design lifecycle. His expertise in mixed-methods research, design systems, rapid prototyping, and even leveraging AI tools are directly relevant to the job's requirements. The mention of 'Leading Others' capacity also resonates with Michael's experience mentoring designers. The remote work arrangement is highly preferred by Michael. jobs.jobDescription: FingerCheck is seeking a Remote Senior Product Designer to serve as a strategic leader within their design team, responsible for shaping the user experience across their entire product suite. This role involves close collaboration with Product Management and Engineering to tackle complex problems, champion user needs, and uphold design excellence. The ideal candidate will have 5+ years of experience in Product Design, with a proven track record in B2B SaaS or Fintech, demonstrating the ability to simplify complex, data-heavy applications. This includes experience in mentoring junior designers and facilitating communication between Product and Engineering. Key responsibilities encompass leading data-informed design processes, conducting mixed-methods research, contributing to and evolving the FingerCheck Design System, and driving iterative innovation through rapid prototyping and usability testing. The role also requires proficiency in creating low and high-fidelity interactive wireframes and prototypes, and the ability to integrate Generative AI tools into the design workflow.

jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
jobs.fitExplanation: This role is a strong fit for Michael Crane. The title 'Senior Product Designer' aligns with his target roles. The industry focus on B2B SaaS, enterprise software, and data/analytics within property and finance sectors directly matches Michael's extensive experience and deep context in complex B2B SaaS products, particularly in data-heavy interfaces and transactional workflows. CoreLogic, being an acquired and publicly traded company, far exceeds the Series C minimum stage requirement. The remote location preference is also met. The job responsibilities, emphasizing leadership in full design lifecycle from 0-to-1 discovery to scaled products, user research, design systems, and cross-functional collaboration, are all areas where Michael excels and seeks to apply his skills. His background in visual systems and craft, which is valued in making complex systems 'delightful and tactile,' is highly relevant to designing intuitive data platforms.
jobs.jobDescription:
CoreLogic is seeking a Senior Product Designer to join our Enterprise Data Solutions team. This role involves leading the design of complex B2B SaaS platforms that provide property data, analytics, and workflow automation for financial institutions and real estate professionals. You will be instrumental in making intricate data systems intuitive and enhancing transactional experiences within our enterprise software. This includes end-to-end design from ambiguous problem spaces (0-to-1) to shipped products, focusing on user research, journey mapping, and developing robust design systems. Collaboration with product management and engineering leads is central, as is a commitment to visual craft and interaction polish to build user trust in high-stakes environments.

Core Responsibilities:
*   Lead the full design lifecycle for enterprise data and workflow products, from discovery to delivery.
*   Conduct user research and synthesize insights to define product strategy and user needs.
*   Design complex data-heavy interfaces, transactional flows, and reporting tools for B2B SaaS.
*   Develop and maintain scalable design systems and component libraries in Figma.
*   Collaborate closely with product managers, engineering, and business stakeholders.
*   Advocate for user-centered design, accessibility, and visual craft within the product.
*   Contribute to shaping design strategy and mentorship within the team.

Key Requirements:
*   10+ years of product design experience, with a strong focus on B2B SaaS or enterprise software.
*   Portfolio demonstrating expertise in complex system design, data visualization, and transactional workflows.
*   Proficiency with Figma for UI design, wireframing, and prototyping.
*   Experience with user research methodologies (e.g., affinity mapping, user flows, journey mapping).
*   Strong understanding of design systems and their implementation at scale.
*   Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
*   Experience in fintech, real estate tech, or related data-intensive industries is a plus.

jobs.jobDescription:
GameChanger is seeking a Senior Product Designer to join their remote team. The designer will be responsible for creating intuitive and engaging experiences for their software platform. This role involves the full design lifecycle, from user research and conceptualization to prototyping and contributing to the design system. The ideal candidate will be adept at collaborating with product managers and engineers to deliver impactful product features.

Core Responsibilities:
*   Lead the design of new features and improvements for the core product.
*   Conduct user research to inform design decisions and validate concepts.
*   Develop wireframes, flows, and high-fidelity prototypes.
*   Collaborate closely with product management and engineering teams.
*   Contribute to the evolution and maintenance of the design system.

Requirements:
*   7+ years of experience in product design, with a strong portfolio showcasing user-centered design solutions.
*   Proven ability to work effectively in a remote, cross-functional environment.
*   Proficiency in design and prototyping tools (e.g., Figma, Sketch).

jobs.jobDescription:
NinjaTrader is looking for a Staff UX Product Designer to contribute to their trading platform. The role involves designing intuitive and efficient user experiences for complex financial tools, catering to traders and financial professionals. The designer will be responsible for end-to-end design, from research and ideation to prototyping and implementation support. Collaboration with product management and engineering is key to delivering high-quality, impactful features in a fast-paced fintech environment.

Core Responsibilities:
*   Lead UX design for features within a fintech trading platform.
*   Conduct user research to understand the needs of traders and financial users.
*   Create wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity designs for complex financial workflows.
*   Collaborate closely with product managers, engineers, and stakeholders to define and deliver product solutions.
*   Contribute to and maintain a scalable design system for consistency across the platform.

Requirements:
*   8+ years of product design experience, ideally within fintech, financial services, or other complex enterprise software domains.
*   Proven ability to design for data-heavy interfaces and transactional flows.
*   Expertise in user research, information architecture, and interaction design.
*   Proficiency with modern design tools (e.g., Figma).
*   Strong communication and collaboration skills to work with cross-functional teams.

jobs.jobDescription:
Netomi is seeking a Staff Product Designer to own complex, foundational initiatives on its AI-first enterprise customer experience platform. The designer will be responsible for leading the design of core platform elements, starting with the customer-facing control tower for AI agent management. This role involves connecting product strategy to elegant UX, transforming ambiguous and technically deep problem spaces into intuitive experiences. The ideal candidate will drive the transition to a product-led growth (PLG) model through self-serve onboarding and in-product education.

Core Responsibilities:
* Own end-to-end design for platform-foundation initiatives, from strategy to polished UX.
* Translate ambiguous, technical problem spaces into coherent user experiences.
* Drive product-led growth by designing self-serve onboarding and education.
* Shape the core of the AI agent management platform.

Key Requirements:
* 8–12 years of product design experience, particularly in complex B2B/enterprise SaaS.
* Proven ownership of large, ambiguous initiatives from zero to shipped.
* Strong systems thinking to create coherent frameworks.
* Curiosity about customers, AI, systems, and business operations.
* Experience with AI, data, developer tools, or workflow products is a plus.
